items.title: Watch: Match of the Day
items.summary: Highlights from nine Premier League games from a busy midweek schedule.

items.title: Man City latest: Pep gives update on Haaland fitness after early sub
Fan group: Ratcliffe immigrant views 'concerning'
items.summary: A Manchester United supporters group condemned comments made by part-owner Sir Jim Ratcliffe after the British billionaire said the UK has been "colonised" by immigrants.

items.title: Miami friendly in PR postponed after Messi injury
items.summary: Inter Miami CF's friendly with Independiente del Valle in Puerto Rico has been postponed for two weeks after Lionel Messi sustained a muscle strain in his left hamstring during its last preseason game against Barcelona SC on Saturday.

items.title: Sources: Leagues Cup to play in Mexican venues
items.summary: Leagues Cup officials are finalizing the details to have Mexico host certain matches of the upcoming edition of the tournament, sources told ESPN.

items.title: Haaland gives Man City injury scare after HT sub
items.summary: Erling Haaland has given Manchester City an injury scare after Pep Guardiola revealed the striker asked to be substituted during the 3-0 win over Fulham.

items.title: USWNT's Bethune off to KC, Hutton joins Bay FC
items.summary: United States midfielder Croix Bethune has joined the Kansas City Current, and USWNT midfielder Claire Hutton has joined Bay FC, in a pair of separate seven-figure moves, multiple sources have confirmed to ESPN.

items.title: Geen zorgen bij Schreuder na onverwachte nederlaag NEC
items.summary: Dick Schreuder maakt zich allesbehalve zorgen na de verrassende nederlaag van NEC tegen FC Utrecht. Na maanden van euforie en positiviteit diende de ploeg van Ron Jans de Nijmegenaren de eerste nederlaag sinds 2 november 2025 toe. Ook toen was Utrecht overigens de tegenstander.

items.title: Late own goal helps Aston Villa beat Brighton
items.summary: A late Jack Hinshelwood own goal gives Aston Villa a 1-0 win over Brighton, in a game where their former midfielder James Milner equals the all-time Premier League appearance record.

items.title: Burnley score three quickfire goals to stun Crystal Palace in comeback win
items.summary: Burnley score three goals in seven minutes, as Scott Parker's side come from two goals down to beat Crystal Palace 3-2 at Selhurst Park in the Premier League.

items.title: Van Dijk scores as Liverpool beat Sunderland to close top four gap
items.summary: Virgil Van Dijk's second half-header gives Liverpool a 1-0 win over Sunderland, as Arne Slot's side become the first team to win away at the Stadium of Light in the Premier League this season.

items.title: Slot telt zegeningen na eerste uitzege in 2026 en heeft slecht nieuws over Endo
items.summary: Grote opluchting bij Arne Slot na de winst van Liverpool op Sunderland. Zijn ploeg verkeert niet in de beste fase, maar de nederlaag tegen Manchester City is in ieder geval wel weggepoetst.

items.title: Van Dijk kopt zich geschiedenisboeken van Liverpool in: 'Een mooie stap voorwaarts
items.summary: Virgil van Dijk heeft woensdagavond een plek in de geschiedenisboeken van Liverpool veroverd. Door te scoren tegen Sunderland, werd de 34-jarige Nederlander niet alleen matchwinner, maar maakte hij bovenal zijn 23ste Premier League-doelpunt in dienst van The Reds. Daarmee is Van Dijk nu de meest scorende verdediger aller tijden van Liverpool.

items.title: Euforie bij FC Utrecht: 'Begon bijna lachwekkend te worden'
items.summary: Er werd woensdag eindelijk weer eens gelachen bij FC Utrecht. De ploeg van trainer Ron Jans won verrassend op bezoek bij nummer drie NEC: 1-3. Het was de eerste zege in de Eredivisie sinds 9 november 2025, toen Ajax werd verslagen.

items.title: Engelse media zien Van Dijk opstaan: 'Dat verdient lof'
items.summary: Engelse media zijn woensdagavond onder de indruk van Liverpool, dat op bezoek bij Sunderland een nipte zege boekte. De ploeg van Arne Slot overtuigde weer eens en dat oogst lof van de pers.

items.title: Haaland ends open-play goal drought as Man City beat Fulham
items.summary: Erling Haaland scores his first goal from open play in the Premier League in nine games, as Manchester City soundly defeat Fulham 3-0 to move three points behind league leaders Arsenal.

items.title: Virgil van Dijk rises to occasion as Liverpool end Sunderland record
items.summary: Arne Slot has said he does not believe his job security hinges on Liverpool securing Champions League qualification this spring but the Dutchman would much prefer not to put that assertion to the test. On this evidence there seems a decent chance the Champions’s manager will not have to. In finally ending Sunderland’s proud unbeaten home record in the Premier League this season, sixth placed Liverpool left themselves only three points adrift of fourth placed Manchester United and two behind fifth placed Chelsea. Continue reading...

items.title: Lucca howler is worst of bad bunch for Forest against Wolves as Dyche feels heat
items.summary: Evangelos Marinakis often takes action when the crowd turns against a head coach. The Nottingham Forest owner watched from his throne, enduring 90 minutes of inept finishing and sloppy play in a desperate goalless draw against rock‑bottom Wolves, resulting in boos and insults aimed at Sean Dyche at full time. Thirty-five shots came and went for Forest, few testing José Sá but the patience of the crowd was thoroughly tested. It was a missed opportunity for the hosts, who moved only one point further north of 18th-placed West Ham, when three were there for the taking. Continue reading...

items.title: Milner reaches milestone but Brighton’s misery goes on after late Villa strike
items.summary: On a night when James Milner made history, equalling his former teammate Gareth Barry’s Premier League appearance record, his former club, Aston Villa, created more unwanted headlines for Fabian ­Hürzeler. A Tyrone Mings header, deflected in off Jack Hinshelwood with four ­minutes of the 90 to play, was ­sufficient to down Brighton and extend their miserable run. It said everything about a poor Villa display that Unai Emery was unmoved when the goal went in, though he could not conceal his delight at victory and hitting the 50-point mark with 12 games to go. Continue reading...

items.title: Manchester City make quick work of Fulham to cut Arsenal’s lead at the top
items.summary: Manchester City’s week is moving along sweetly, this win closing the gap to three points to Arsenal, who travel to Brentford on Thursday. On Sunday, they defeated Liverpool at Anfield, on Wednesday they downed Fulham at home to reel off a 20th consecutive victory against them. The rosiest moment for the title challengers was Erling Haaland’s 39th‑minute strike, a first in the competition from open play in nine games, though more concerning was his removal at the break, when City led 3-0. Continue reading...
